transcript.clean_text: coming up on this week's gcn racing news show crashes crosswinds and climbs all feature in the opening week of the gerald italia but the gc is still wide open we'll be taking a look at five key things we've learned from the opening nine stages but we'll also look back at the women's one day races from spain controversial sprint finishes at the tour of hungary another early celebration this time at tro bro leon the majorca challenge races and celebrate the return of tom demoula first up though what have we learned from the first week of the dear italia well number one egan bernal is back and his back is holding up the colombian came into the race as ineos grenadier's co-leader with pavel sivakov but you'd imagine he'd be their sole leader by now even if the russian hadn't crashed out on stage four burnout has looked back to his best and certainly the most sprightly and informed of any of the gc favorites on stage four he bridged across to an attack by mikhail lander and he made that look easy on stage six he gapped a lot of his key rivals in the gc on the climb to the finish despite a headwind and then yesterday he took the stage win and the leader's pink jersey i thought it was more of a left jab than a complete knockout blow but the way he springs to the line in the big ring certainly sent out a very clear message to his rivals he's shown himself to be the man to beat even if we are yet to hit the very high mountains he is only the fifth colombian rider to wear the malia rosa at the giron italia one of the other four nara quintana currently remains the only other colombian winner we've got a long way still to go to milan of course but bernal is looking good to become the second the question remains though as to how his back is going to hold out over three weeks but so far so good we also learned that remco avenue never ceases to surprise even if nothing he does is ever a surprise and yes i know that makes no sense but you know what i mean as things stand he is burnout's closest rival on the general classification and i'd say he's ridden a relatively understated but also very solid race so far and looks like he'll head into the first rest day sitting in second place overall not bad for a 21 year old riding his first grand tour after nine months off through injury now we understand from an article written last week by his team boss patrick lefevre that he's currently four and a half kilograms lighter than when he won his last race the tour of poland last august and given that he was 65 kilograms before that would represent an increase of around half a watt per kilo at threshold if he's managed to maintain the same power now i'd say that we can expect the unexpected when the race heads into the high mountains but without nepal nothing is really unexpected or indeed surprising what we do know is that he is going into the unknown he's never done so many back-to-back days of racing as he's done by this point and we're not even halfway through the race just yet we're also reminded unfortunately that crashes are always going to be part and parcel of pro racing whatever safety measures are put into place over the coming years if you've got 180 cyclists in close proximity at warp speed there are always going to be some unfortunate incidents stage four saw two of those and it took four riders out of the race pavel sivakov was one of those he crashed alone but then in the closing kilometers three riders crashed into a marshall warning of a traffic island and all three went home francois beda plus stage winner joe dombrowski and one of the favourites for pink mikhail lander his team bahrain victorious managed to bounce back from that disappointment the very next day with gino maida taking the win but then stage nine saw this very nasty fool for mati maharaj he got things uncharacteristically wrong there going into a corner on an early descent locking up the back wheel and hitting the inside curb which basically high sided him off his bike i think we all feared the worst for him but thankfully he got off relatively lightly he took to social media last night to say that it was his bike and helmet that took most of the impact no broken bones and only minor concussion so all things considered i'd definitely say he's got off lightly which we can all be very thankful for get well soon mate now one crash that should never have happened though was this one from stage six the bike exchange team car driving into the back of peter serry of the kernick quickstep now there were people who said it was a consequence of those new uci rules which forbid riders throwing their jackets to the side of the road apparently they were just trying to get one back from the commissar's car but i'm sorry that was completely inexcusable the drivers of the team cars should be keeping their eyes on the road at all times whatever the circumstances that driver in this case was gene bates and i know he would have been incredibly upset with himself for that instant but i think it served as a lesson to him and indeed to all the other riders in the convoy of course mistakes can happen but i do feel like this one could have been avoided bates was sent home from the race and matt white who was in the passenger seat given a 2 000 swiss franc fine thankfully siri wasn't too badly injured and was able to continue with the race and he graciously accepted bike exchange's apology afterwards number four then attila valtter is now a name that we all know he became the first hungarian ever to wear the pink jersey when he went into it at the end of stage six at just 22 years of age he is yet another bright young prospect that will no doubt be in the fight for some major wins over the next few years he spent last season with team ccc before that he'd won the queen stage of the tour de lavenier back in 2019 and he's another rider who spent his formative years competing off-road in his instance in cross-country mountain biking i've said it before i'll say it again it's a very exciting time to be a fan of pro cycling i guess the same to a degree goes for victor lafayette of team coffidis and tucker vanderhorn of antomachi wonti gobert names that most of us would not have been that familiar with before the start of the race but names we know a lot about now i always love those new stories that write themselves during the grand tours and finally we learned that you should never count out the early breakaway borah hands grow row tirelessly to try and set up peter sagan for victory back on stage three but as mentioned taco vanderhorn made a name for himself and he'd had other ideas he had adopted almost every era advantage he could with narrow bars skin suits and tape over his helmet vents amongst other things but it was his legs that did most of the talking and they helped him to his and his team's biggest ever win that was definitely one of my favorite moments of the race so far it's a sport that throws up so many nice surprises isn't it constantly okay we will move on away from the gerald italian now and over to the basque country the women's peloton reconvened there last week for a series of one day races the first of which was the omakami nafareoki clasicoa a race that was held on some pretty torrid conditions but that doesn't seem to matter in terms of what anna meek van fluerten can achieve she made multiple attacks and finally managed to go clear the rest of the field on the final climb with just over four k's to go and i'd say she was never seen again before the line but she was demi vollering came agonisingly close but couldn't quite catch her before they got there the following day we had the navara women's elite classic and there was aliso longo borghini and mavi garcia who animated the final they were caught with just over two k's to go q attack from phantom fleurton but on this occasion she didn't quite have it all her own way she was caught in the closing kilometer by ruth winder and arlene sierra and it was the cuban national champion the team armonix who took the win her 37th as a pro and i always loved seeing arlene's winning because her backstory is just so humbling and inspiring have a read online if you ever get the chance and you haven't done so already it was turn of the world champion yesterday though anna van der bregen winning the grand prime yosea dad de aiba coming home solo 16 seconds clear of van fluerten with longo borghini in third i did love this tweet from elisa longo borghini after the stage yesterday though a lady asked her for a photo but as she went to pose for it she then realized that lady was in fact asking her if she'd be kind enough to take a photo of the fan with van der bregen ouch meanwhile up in brittany it was the 36th edition of trobro leon kind of the original modern gravel race if that's not a contradiction in terms the gravel tracks there though are far rougher than those that we see each year estrade bianca less roads and more farm tracks i think 26 of them in total over the course of a 207 kilometer race now it's a race that i caught up with last night and you can too on gcm plus if you so wish but if not a short summary is coming now so the winning move went inside the closing 25ks and it would ultimately be a group of four who would contest the win despite the best efforts of uno x's rasmus tiller who made contact with them just as they started the sprints that are lying raising his arms aloft in celebration as he crossed the line was connor swift of archaea samsic the only trouble was he'd celebrated too early queue a long and agonizing weight as the uci commerce says studied the photo finish i think we've been given third place for baptist blanca he's got it conor swift is your winner biggest win of his professional career you can see what it means to the yorkshire man look at that the ikea samsung team there to celebrate as well in good number yes thankfully for swift his wheel had just about crossed the line in front of pete allegates the belgian rider from team coffidus and another belgian baptist playing card of antomache wantigo bear rounded out the podium in third that marks the biggest win of swift's career since he beat oliver bridgewood in a 10 mile time trail last winter oh lovely it was also the first time there has been no frenchman on the podium of trobro leon since the last time it took place in 2019 hashtag stats killian kelly would be proud another just slightly more interesting stat is that it had been 18 years since felipe gilbert had competed at the race he finished second to samuel de moulan all the way back in 2003 which was a year i took part in that race believe it or not as your bear could only manage 11th yesterday that is a long old career though isn't it i mean 2003 feels like a lifetime ago and i must admit i hadn't realized that this is his 19th year as a pro and by the time he retires at the end of next season he'll have spent longer as a pro rider than not remarkable right maybe that stat wasn't any better either so i'm going to quickly move on to the tour of hungary four of the five stages there finished in bunch sprints phil bauhaus of bahrain victorious one on days one and three yori mus or bora hansgro on stage two and then another belgian and tunes of trek sega fredo on the fifth and final day that sprint though was slightly controversial if you just look a little further back rudy barbier taking his hands off the bars and literally pushing mike journalism to the right at over 60 kilometers per hour turnerson as you would expect was still furious about the incident hours later but barbier then replied to him and accused him of acting like an eight-year-old on twitter time to get the popcorn out at that point i thought but in all seriousness again no excuse for that kind of behaviour in a sprint and once again it is going to be interesting to see whether the uci take any action as they have done recently incidentally by handing a two-month ban from competition to nasa buhari for that swerve that put jake stewart into the barriers at their grand prix paid la lawar back in march right back to the racing though an overall classification was decided on stage four kakaroo doing a great job in setting up their climber garcia who attacked with just under 1500 metres remaining and it looked to be job done until damian house and took flight from behind catching and passing the colombian who would have to settle for fourth behind houston herman's and 19 year old antonio tiberi of trek segafredo and that was how the overall classification would also look after that fifth and final stage my favorite moment of the race though was this one rudiger select checking his teammate yordy muse for signs of concussion after he crashed on stage three now selig is the same rider who rode back to check august jensen who crashed at the skeleta praise last year and the same man who checked on marcel alago after a crash in the 2015 duas door of landron cycling needs more people like you rudiger right we are going to move on again to what's coming up on gcn plus now of course the jira will resume on wednesday after its first rest day and it's not a stage you want to miss the strategy bianca stage to montalcino with 39k of gravel road in the final part of the stage beyond the gear in italia though we also got plenty of other racing again wednesday also marks the start of the welter and lucille where we'll see the likes of lopez hater christoph pedersen cavendish and greipel competing uh van der bregen and fan flute will be back in action at the walter burgos feminas starting on the same day and then on saturday we've got the tour de finistere sunday the welter mercier and then on monday it is the mercantile classic out through mala team now due to the sheer volume of racing we've got going on at the moment i'm afraid there will not be short highlights available for those races but we will have them available on demand for you to watch at your convenience as ever territory restrictions do apply so please check what's available where you are meanwhile pippa york will be our guest on the world of cycling sofa this week the next episode will be out tomorrow tuesday so is to give you something to watch whilst the jira takes a one-day break psy and pippa will be looking back at the best of the action from the first week of the jiro amongst other things and stay tuned tomorrow for the uci track champions league official launch and presentation uh chris hilly will be right here in this very studio talking about this brand new event with orla shenry and you'll also hear from the other event ambassador christina vogel it's going to be live streamed on g sim plus as well as jesus racing's youtube and facebook channels from 10 am bst so that's 11 am cest or you'll be able to watch it on demand after that too right just before we get on to the other racing related news from last week we've got more racing uh four days in fact of it over at the new yorker challenge normally held in january but postponed this year until just last week now again if you'd like to catch up with those races they are available as short highlights on demand on gcm plus but i'll give you the results now in the middle two days were the hardest really daya and puerto polenso the finished venues for them they were won by jesus harada and winner anna kona respectively ryan gibbons had won the first stage in a two-hour sprint with anthony de la place whilst the final day into port dalcudia was won in the sprint finish by andre greipel the gorilla is back it's been almost two and a half years since he last got to celebrate a win that was at the tropical and misa bongo in january of 2019 but yesterday he racked up his 157th career victory coming to the line ahead of alexander kristoff great to see him back now in other news it's also great to see tom demouland back the dutchman made that surprise announcement that he was taking a break from the sport just before the start of this season and neither him nor us knew if he'd come back the good news though is that his team jumbo visme announced last week he'll make his competitive return at the tour de swiss which starts just one week after the jira italia concludes now it appears unlikely that he'll be back at the tour de france this year given his lack of racing firstly but also the fact they've already announced their squad for that race but you would imagine the olympic games is going to be a big goal for him either way just great to see him return from the sidelines isn't it last time he did that he won the gyro natalya if you know what i mean his teammate what vernat has had his preparations delayed for the tour de france he's been forced to take some time away from training as he's recovering from appendicitis although knowing him his recovery won't take particularly long and finally the second round of the mountain bike world cup took place at the weekend and it was tom pidcock showing everyone else how it was done he won the men's race by a full minute over matcha vanderpool and that marked the first british win in a men's cross country world cup for 27 years meanwhile in the women's race it was lyanna lecompte who took her second straight world cup victory in the space of just eight days this time beating haley batten of the usa the 22 year old instantly had won the short track race on friday right that's all for this week there's been a lot to get through hopefully we'll have your company for our live jira natalia coverage for the rest of this week and if you can only watch one of this week's stages live make sure it's wednesdays it's going to be quite the spectacle hopefully see you then goodbye
